generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Converts electrical current into mechanical torque for the robot's first axis"
]

instance GearWheelTransmission : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Transforms torque and angular velocity to achieve desired motion in the gear transmission system"
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides high torque transmission with compact design for the robot's first axis"
]

instance SINUMERIK840DslCNCController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controls the motor and axes based on input commands (position, speed, or acceleration) for precise motion execution"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures the motor's angular position and velocity for feedback control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures the output angular position and velocity of the gearbox to enable backlash and elasticity compensation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures the input current to the motor, used to derive torque via motor constants"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLStructuralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram and Internal Block Diagram describing the robot's first axis (motor, gearboxes, connectors)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler, AssetAdministrationShellEnabler
]

instance SimulinkFrictionlessModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink model representing frictionless behavior of the motor-gearbox system"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance SimulinkCoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction characteristics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance SimulinkStribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink model implementing Stribeck friction characteristics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance SimulinkDahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink model implementing Dahl friction characteristics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance SimulinkLuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink model implementing LuGre friction characteristics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance DynamicsTorqueDerivationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model to derive torque from current and angular data using motor constants"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance MeasuredMotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time measured input current (I_M) to the motor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance MeasuredMotorEncoderData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time measured motor angular position and velocity (r_M)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance MeasuredSecondaryEncoderData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time measured output angular position and velocity (r_out) from the gearbox"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance DerivedMotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Computed torque derived from motor current using motor constants"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MeasuredMotorCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance DerivedAngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Computed angular velocity from encoder data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MeasuredMotorEncoderData, MeasuredSecondaryEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance SimulatedFrictionlessTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated torque output from frictionless model (δ_r_M)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulinkFrictionlessModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance SimulatedCoulombViscousTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated torque output from Coulomb-Viscous friction model (δ_r_G)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulinkCoulombViscousFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance SimulatedStribeckTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated torque output from Stribeck friction model"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulinkStribeckFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance SimulatedDahlTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated torque output from Dahl friction model"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulinkDahlFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance SimulatedLuGreTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated torque output from LuGre friction model"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulinkLuGreFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance FrictionModelMetadata : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Metadata containing friction model parameters and operational conditions (temperature, load)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based modeling environment for structural and logical architecture description"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelingService, MultiPhysicsAnalysisService
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment for behavioral and simulation modeling"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelingService, MultiPhysicsAnalysisService
]

instance MQTTDataStreamingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol for real-time sensor data streaming from physical components to digital shadow"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataVisualizationService
]

instance AssetAdministrationShellEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell for semantic linking of data to physical asset models"
    DTDFVocab:enables TraceabilityService
]

instance ModelBasedSystemsEngineeringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Based Systems Engineering methodology for system description and analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables ContinuousImprovementService
]

instance UnifiedDashboardEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unified visualization dashboard displaying data traces and comparisons (measured vs. simulated)"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataVisualizationService
]

instance DataIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Integration layer combining sensor data, simulated data, and derived quantities"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelingService, TraceabilityService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Combines white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models to continuously identify friction behavior"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionIdentificationInsight, ModelRefinementAction
]

instance RealTimeDataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Displays data traces (measured vs. simulated) in a unified dashboard for monitoring and analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceVisualizationInsight
]

instance TraceabilityService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Links data points to their sources (sensors, simulations) and models for explainability"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataSourceTraceabilityInsight
]

instance MultiPhysicsAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integrates structural (SysML) and behavioral (Simulink) models to understand physical relationships (e.g., torque, angular velocity)"
    DTDFVocab:provides PhysicsRelationshipInsight
]

instance ContinuousImprovementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables dynamic exchange of friction models to adapt to changes (wear, tooling, applications)"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelExchangeRecommendationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionIdentificationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of static, kinetic, and viscous friction components in the gearbox"
]

instance ModelRefinementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Continuous improvement of friction models based on real-time data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DataTraceVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual comparison of measured vs. simulated data traces for monitoring and analysis"
]

instance DataSourceTraceabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured linking of data points to their sources (sensors, simulations) for explainability"
]

instance PhysicsRelationshipInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Understanding of physical relationships between torque, angular velocity, and system components"
]

instance ModelExchangeRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommendations for dynamic exchange of friction models to adapt to operational changes"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ControlParameterAdjustmentRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommendations for adjusting CNC controller parameters to compensate for friction"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance WearAnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of wear or anomalies (e.g., unexpected friction spikes) triggering maintenance or recalibration"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The interactions from the virtual world to the physical world are **indirect** in this Digital Shadow implementation, as it is a **one-way data flow** (passive entity). However, the insights gained from the digital shadow (e.g., friction model improvements) can be used to:
  - **Update control algorithms**: Improve robot motion accuracy by refining friction compensation in the CNC controller.
  - **Parameter adjustments**: Adjust friction model parameters in real-time to account for wear, temperature, or load changes.
  - **No direct actuation**: The digital shadow does not autonomously send commands back to the physical system; instead, it provides data-driven insights for human or automated decision-making."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital shadow operates in **real-time** with the following characteristics:
  - **Live data processing**: Sensor data is streamed and processed continuously during robot operation.
  - **Dynamic model updates**: Friction models are refined in real-time as new data is collected.
  - **Synchronization**: Data traces are time-aligned with the physical system’s state (e.g., via timestamps in MQTT streams).
  - **No latency-critical feedback**: Since the digital shadow is passive, there is no strict requirement for sub-millisecond synchronization with the physical system."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation consists of:
  - **Physical system**: MABI MAX 100 robot’s first axis (motor, gearboxes, encoders, CNC controller).
  - **Digital shadow components**:
    - **Data sources**: Sensors (encoders, current sensor) streaming via MQTT.
    - **Models**: SysML structural models + Simulink behavioral models.
    - **Processing**: Simulation and data calculation models (e.g., friction models) executed in Simulink.
    - **Visualization**: Dashboard displaying data traces and comparisons.
  - **Orchestration**:
    - Centralized digital shadow for the first axis.
    - One-way data flow from physical to virtual (no feedback loop).
    - Traceability links between data, models, and sources."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital shadow covers the following lifecycle phases of the physical system:
  - **As operated**: Primarily focuses on the robot during **runtime** to monitor and analyze friction in real-time.
  - **Partial as-designed representation**: Uses structural models (SysML) to represent the ideal mechanical structure but does not simulate the 'as-designed' ideal behavior without friction corrections.
  - **No explicit 'as-manufactured' phase**: Does not explicitly model deviations from design specifications (e.g., manufacturing tolerances) but adapts to operational changes (wear, tooling)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are addressed through:
  - **Model fidelity**:
    - Structural models (SysML) accurately represent the physical components (motor, gearboxes).
    - Behavioral models (Simulink) include multiple friction models (white-box and black-box) for comprehensive analysis.
  - **Validation mechanisms**:
    - Comparison of simulated data (with/without friction) against measured data to identify discrepancies.
    - Traceability of data sources and computation methods ensures reproducibility.
  - **Uncertainty and errors**:
    - Uncertainties in friction models are mitigated by combining analytical and data-driven approaches.
    - Sensor noise and backlash are accounted for via dual encoders (motor + secondary).
    - No explicit error propagation analysis is documented, but the methodology supports it via traceability."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Technical connections include:
  - **Network protocols**:
    - MQTT for real-time sensor data streaming from the robot to the digital shadow.
    - Implicit use of OPC UA or similar industrial protocols (not explicitly mentioned but likely for CNC integration).
  - **Architectures**:
    - Centralized digital shadow system with a dashboard for visualization.
    - Simulink models run locally or on a connected server for processing.
    - No cloud-based deployment mentioned; likely on-premise."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Hosting and deployment details:
  - **Local deployment**: Digital shadow and dashboard are likely hosted on-premise (e.g., on a server connected to the robot).
  - **Simulation environment**: Simulink models are executed in a local or networked simulation environment.
  - **No cloud mention**: No reference to cloud hosting or distributed computing."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration includes:
  - **External information systems**:
    - Integration with the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller for control parameter updates.
    - Potential linkage with other digital twins or manufacturing systems (not explicitly detailed).
  - **Data exchange**:
    - MQTT streams sensor data to the digital shadow.
    - No mention of integration with ERP, MES, or other enterprise systems."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership and privacy considerations:
  - **Ownership**: Data is owned by the **robot operator/manufacturer** (RWTH Aachen University or MABI Robotic AG), as the digital shadow is created for internal research and improvement purposes.
  - **Privacy**:
    - No sensitive personal data is involved; data pertains to robot operation and friction analysis.
    - Access control is implied (e.g., dashboard access restricted to authorized personnel).
    - No explicit GDPR or compliance details are provided."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Standards followed:
  - **Modeling**: SysML (ISO 19505) for system architecture.
  - **Simulation**: MathWorks Simulink (de facto standard for control and simulation).
  - **Data communication**: MQTT (lightweight protocol for IoT/data streaming).
  - **Asset administration**: Asset Administration Shell (AAS) based on IEC 62541 (OPC UA) or similar industrial standards.
  - **No explicit DT standardization**: Follows a custom digital shadow reference model but aligns with general MBSE and IoT practices."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety considerations:
  - **Cybersecurity**:
    - MQTT streams are likely secured (e.g., TLS/SSL encryption for data in transit).
    - No mention of authentication/authorization for dashboard access.
  - **Safety**:
    - The digital shadow is **passive** (no direct control of the robot), so no risk of accidental physical harm.
    - Insights are used to improve safety indirectly (e.g., by reducing motion inaccuracies).
    - No remote operation of the robot by the digital twin; all actions require human oversight or pre-approved control logic.
    - Safety cell restricts robot movement to [−90°, 90°], ensuring physical safety during operation."
]
